comment:
QA review: RECOMMEND.

Verified items:
- Frontmatter title: 'Flow State Requirements Traceability Matrix' (present, non-empty).
- Frontmatter revision_summary: present, descriptive, references parent CR-111, names the qualified baseline (flow-state/main@a26f7fb) and System Release (FLOW-STATE-1.0).
- Document structure: Sections 1-6 present (Purpose, Scope, Traceability Summary, Verification Evidence, Qualified Baseline, References) plus END OF DOCUMENT marker.
- Traceability one-to-one with SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.0 (EFFECTIVE): all 12 requirements across the 5 domain groups (ARCH:4, CAD:3, SIM:2, UI:2, APP:1) appear in the Section 3 summary and have dedicated Section 4 evidence subsections.
- Qualified Baseline (Section 5): commit hash 'a26f7fb' is an actual value, NOT 'TBD' or any placeholder. Repository, branch, RS version, System Release tag, and verification method are all populated.
- Verification method ('qualitative-proof inspection') is consistent with the SDLC-CQ-RTM precedent and is appropriate given flow-state has no automated test suite at v1.0.
- Evidence sections cite specific files and (in many cases) explicit line ranges, satisfying the traceability standard for inspection-based verification.
- Self-disclosed asymmetries are explicit: (a) interaction_data placement on Sketch.interaction_data (REQ-FS-ARCH-003), (b) outdated input-handler layer-ordering comment (REQ-FS-UI-002, follow-up CR queued). These are appropriate scope-integrity disclosures rather than concealment.
- Placeholder content scan: none found (no TBD/TODO/FIXME/XXX in document body).
- Parent CR-111 confirmed IN_EXECUTION; SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.0 confirmed EFFECTIVE; the RS/RTM coordination prerequisite for routing is met.

Reviewers assigned: tu_scene, tu_sketch, tu_sim, tu_ui.

Note on assignment: tu_input is not a valid assignee in this project (only tu_ui/tu_scene/tu_sketch/tu_sim are configured as TUs). REQ-FS-UI-002 (Input Chain of Responsibility) is therefore covered by tu_ui along with REQ-FS-UI-001.

QA review focuses on process, scope, traceability, and evidence form. Technical adequacy of the inspection narratives (e.g., whether each cited file/line range substantiates its requirement) is the domain TUs' call.

TU-UI review of UI-domain evidence at qualified baseline flow-state@a26f7fb. Verified the following: (1) REQ-FS-UI-001 — OverlayProvider protocol confirmed at ui_widgets.py:31, Dropdown implementation at line 712, UIManager register/unregister/_draw_overlays methods present at ui_manager.py:301-330; protocol-based overlay rendering accurately described. (2) REQ-FS-UI-002 — handle_input() at input_handler.py:108-129 confirmed with the exact 4-layer dispatch order System-Modal-Global-HUD; the deliberate Modal-before-Global comment at lines 117-118 is verified verbatim; modal-stack reset_interaction_state() calls at app_controller.py:59 and :75 confirmed. The RTM's transparent acknowledgement of the stale module docstring at input_handler.py:4-9 (which reads 'Global / Modals' in the wrong order) and the queued follow-up CR is the correct way to handle that documentation drift — runtime behavior is the authoritative truth and matches the requirement. (3) REQ-FS-ARCH-004 (UI-adjacent ToolContext facade) — tool_context.py confirmed; Tool base class at ui/tools.py:73 and the documented self.app = ctx._app backward-compat extraction at line 87 are accurate. (4) REQ-FS-CAD-003 (UI-adjacent Interaction Servo) — sketch.interaction_data at sketch.py:33 confirmed as the buffer used by the SelectTool via ctx.set_interaction_data; the RTM's honest note in REQ-FS-ARCH-003 about interaction_data living on Sketch rather than Session correctly identifies the known asymmetry rather than misrepresenting it. Evidence narratives are specific, file-and-line-anchored, and faithful to the v1.0 source. The RS-RTM mapping is one-to-one across all 12 requirements with no gaps in the UI domain. The two UI requirements (retained tree + overlays; 4-layer input chain) are appropriately scoped for a high-level v1.0 RS; granular UI affordances are correctly deferred to future CRs per the RS purpose statement. Recommend.

Reviewed for the Simulation domain (REQ-FS-SIM-001, REQ-FS-SIM-002) at qualified baseline a26f7fb. Technical claims verify against source: SoA particle arrays at simulation.py:51-69 (correct), kick-drift-kick Verlet in integrate_n_steps (correct), spatial hash O(N) via build_neighbor_list at physics_core.py:48-97 (correct), is_static=3 tethered atom write at compiler.py:236 and is_static=1 static atom write at compiler.py:266 (both correct), Scene.rebuild at core/scene.py:421 dispatching to compiler.rebuild (correct), and the sync_entity_arrays / sync_static_atoms_to_geometry fast path at simulation.py:358-508 (correct). The two-way CAD-Physics coupling via tether forces (apply_tether_forces, simulation.py:427-454) is accurately characterized as the mechanism enabling entity-particle bidirectional influence. Minor non-blocking precision notes (offered for an optional tightening pass, not request-updates): (1) REQ-FS-SIM-001 cites integrate_n_steps as 'lines 100-180' but the function actually runs through line 251; the cited range covers the declaration and first half-kick + drift but stops short of the LJ force loop (~196-233) and the second half-kick (~236-247) that complete the kick-drift-kick cycle; consider 'lines 100-251' or 'lines 100+' so a reader following the citation sees the full integrator. (2) REQ-FS-SIM-001 says arrays are 'preallocated to MAX_ATOMS'; the actual attribute is self.capacity = 5000 (no MAX_ATOMS constant); and atom_color is (capacity, 3) rather than strictly 1-D. The SoA architectural claim is correct; only the prose phrasing is loose. Neither point rises to a defect under the inspection-based verification model — the citations land readers in the right function/file and the architectural properties are correctly stated. Recommending.

TU-SCENE review (orchestration domain). One substantive concern.

REQ-FS-ARCH-001 (Air Gap) evidence overstates 'sole authorized mutation gateway.' The narrative in Section 4.1 states that 'scene.execute(command)' is 'the sole authorized mutation gateway for persistent state' and that 'Cascade effects of commands ... are downstream consequences of authorized commands, not separate state-mutation channels.' This omits a known living asymmetry: Scene.paint_particles() and Scene.erase_particles() (flow-state/core/scene.py lines 452-491) mutate persistent Simulation state directly, bypassing the Command system. They are paired with Simulation.snapshot() per the Physics-Undo-via-Time-Travel pattern documented in CLAUDE.md Section 3.3, and they are deliberately exposed on the ToolContext (paint_particles, erase_particles, snapshot_particles at flow-state/core/tool_context.py lines 383-421). Simulation is persistent state (it serializes into .scn files via Scene.save_scene at scene.py line 650), so this is genuinely a second mutation path for persistent state.

The current evidence text would mislead a reviewer who asks 'is there any path that mutates persistent state without a command?' — the truthful answer is 'yes: particle brush, with snapshot-based undo as the alternative reversibility mechanism.'

Recommendation: Add a sentence (or short paragraph) to the REQ-FS-ARCH-001 evidence acknowledging the particle brush as an authorized exception that uses snapshot-based undo (per CLAUDE.md Section 3.3 'Physics Undo (Time Travel)'), not the command system. This parallels the diligence already shown in the v0.3 revision summary for other known asymmetries (interaction_data on Sketch, input-layer order, static vs. tethered atoms).

All other orchestration-domain claims verified against the qualified-baseline source at flow-state@a26f7fb: Scene.execute at scene.py line 184, Command ABC abstracts at command_base.py, CommandQueue historize handling at commands.py lines 78-110, Scene ownership of sketch/simulation/process_objects/commands at scene.py lines 65-89, ToolContext facade with private _app at tool_context.py, orchestration loop ordering and the constraints-or-interaction_data solver gate at scene.py line 324, Scene.rebuild() at scene.py line 421, _topology_dirty flag at scene.py line 293. Qualified-baseline commit a26f7fb matches actual flow-state/main HEAD with the cited commit message.

Reviewing as TU-SKETCH (CAD/Geometry domain). The qualified-baseline commit a26f7fb is verified, and the architectural invariants (Air Gap, Command Pattern, Scene/Session split, ToolContext facade) are correctly identified. However, three evidence narratives in my domain contain verifiable factual errors that should be corrected before approval, since the RTM is the verification audit trail and inaccurate evidence undermines its purpose:

1) REQ-FS-ARCH-002 (Command Pattern) cites 'DeleteEntityCommand' as a concrete example. No such class exists in the codebase. The actual class is 'RemoveEntityCommand' (model/commands/geometry.py:77). Please replace 'DeleteEntityCommand' with 'RemoveEntityCommand'.

2) REQ-FS-CAD-003 (Interaction Servo) states the Select tool injects mouse drag state via 'ctx.set_interaction_data(...)' each frame. The facade method exists on ToolContext (tool_context.py:427), but tools.py does NOT call it -- it directly mutates 'self.sketch.interaction_data = {...}' (lines 788, 950, 978, 1061). The requirement is still satisfied (the solver does treat the data as an infinite-mass constraint), but the cited injection path is incorrect. Please update the evidence to describe the actual direct-assignment path, or note this as a known asymmetry similar to the interaction_data placement note already in REQ-FS-ARCH-003.

3) REQ-FS-CAD-002 (PBD Solver with Hybrid Backend): minor framing issue. The evidence says 'solve() (the legacy Python/OOP backend) and solve_numba() (the kernel backend)'. In fact, solve() is a dispatcher that takes use_numba as a parameter and delegates to either the inline legacy path or to solve_numba(). The substantive claim (both backends exist, switchable at runtime via F9) is correct. Recommend rephrasing to 'solve() dispatches to either the legacy OOP path (inline) or solve_numba() based on the use_numba flag.'

Minor (non-blocking, illustrative-only): REQ-FS-CAD-001 evidence lists 'equal-radius' and 'distance' constraints as examples; neither exists as a distinct class. The requirement uses 'e.g.' so this is illustrative, but please align the examples with actual classes (Length, Radius, EqualLength, FixedAngle, plus PARALLEL/PERPENDICULAR/HORIZONTAL/VERTICAL via the Angle dispatcher class).

QA pre-review of SDLC-FLOW-RTM v0.1 (inaugural RTM under CR-111). VERIFIED: (1) Frontmatter title='Flow State Requirements Traceability Matrix' is present and non-empty. (2) revision_summary is substantive and accurately describes the v0.2 corrections to v0.1 review feedback (RemoveEntityCommand vs DeleteEntityCommand, Solver.solve as dispatcher, interaction_data direct mutation reality, integrate_n_steps span 100-251, capacity vs MAX_ATOMS, atom_color shape). (3) All required sections present: Purpose, Scope, Traceability Summary, Verification Evidence (per REQ), Qualified Baseline, References. (4) CRITICAL RTM CHECK: Qualified Baseline commit hash is 'a26f7fb' — NOT 'TBD' or placeholder; verified to exist in whharris917/flow-state with matching commit message 'Remove personal note from README' (Feb 5 2026). (5) Companion document SDLC-FLOW-RS v1.0 is EFFECTIVE; authorizing CR-111 is IN_EXECUTION — proper SDLC coordination. (6) All 12 requirements (REQ-FS-ARCH-001..004, REQ-FS-CAD-001..003, REQ-FS-SIM-001..002, REQ-FS-UI-001..002, REQ-FS-APP-001) are traced with PASS results. (7) Spot-checked code claims at the qualified baseline a26f7fb (Scene.execute, paint_particles/erase_particles, ToolContext brush facade, RemoveEntityCommand at geometry.py:77, Solver.solve dispatcher pattern, Simulation SoA layout, integrate_n_steps location): all verified accurate. (8) No placeholder content (no TBD/TODO/FIXME/XXX) found in document body. (9) Intellectual honesty noted and appreciated: REQ-FS-ARCH-001 explicitly documents the brush-operations exception rather than glossing it; REQ-FS-CAD-003 acknowledges the ToolContext facade is unused; REQ-FS-UI-002 flags the stale CLAUDE.md narrative — these honest acknowledgments strengthen the RTM rather than weaken it. ASSIGNED REVIEWERS: tu_scene, tu_sketch, tu_sim, tu_ui — to provide independent technical verification of code claims within their respective domains. QA review: RECOMMEND.

TU-SIM review: RECOMMEND. Independently verified all simulation-domain evidence against source at the qualified baseline. REQ-FS-SIM-001: Confirmed flat-array SoA layout in engine/simulation.py lines 51-69 (capacity=5000 default at line 40, atom_color (capacity,3) at line 60); integrate_n_steps spans engine/physics_core.py:100-251 with the standard velocity-Verlet form; build_neighbor_list at line 49 and spatial_sort at line 254 implement the uniform-grid cell list. REQ-FS-SIM-002: Confirmed engine/compiler.py writes is_static=3 at line 236 (_add_tethered_atom) and is_static=1 at line 266 (_add_static_atom); Scene.rebuild() at core/scene.py:421 triggers compiler.rebuild(); sync_entity_arrays at simulation.py:358 and sync_static_atoms_to_geometry at 456 (cited range 358-508 is accurate). Cross-cutting: REQ-FS-ARCH-001 brush-exception evidence verified — Scene.paint_particles/erase_particles at scene.py:452-491 and ToolContext.paint_particles/erase_particles at tool_context.py:383-413 with snapshot_particles at 415-421. The CLAUDE.md §3.3 framing of brush as a documented authorized exception under the time-travel snapshot pattern is accurate and well-justified. Minor terminology nit (non-blocking): the RTM describes integrate_n_steps as 'velocity Verlet integration in the kick-drift-kick form'. The implementation is standard velocity Verlet (drift + half-kick / force-recompute / half-kick), which is mathematically equivalent to KDK leapfrog but conventionally distinct in nomenclature. Consider simplifying to 'velocity Verlet' in a future revision; not requesting updates. v0.1 corrections (RemoveEntityCommand, capacity vs MAX_ATOMS, integration line range, solve dispatcher framing) are all verified correct.

Evidence in the orchestration domain (core/scene.py, core/command_base.py, core/commands.py, core/tool_context.py, model/commands/geometry.py, app/app_controller.py) is accurate and traceable to verified line numbers and class/file assertions at the qualified-baseline commit a26f7fb. Key checks: Scene.execute() at scene.py:184 is correctly characterized as the principal mutation gateway with CommandQueue delegation and historize-driven stack insertion; the brush exception (paint_particles/erase_particles at scene.py:452-491, exposed on ToolContext at lines 383-413 with snapshot_particles at 415) is genuinely a documented authorized exception, with justification correctly grounded in CLAUDE.md section 3.3 (snapshot-based time-travel undo for irreversible physics); RemoveEntityCommand (model/commands/geometry.py:77, changes_topology=True with serialized-form storage on execute) is correctly named after the v0.2 correction; the Command ABC contract (execute/undo abstract, historize attribute) at command_base.py is accurately described; the Scene/Session split is faithfully reflected (Scene owns sketch/simulation/compiler/command_queue/process_objects; Session owns camera/selection/focused_element/mode/current_tool); the interaction_data asymmetry note under REQ-FS-ARCH-003 is appropriately scoped and the consistent direct-mutation pattern in tools is correctly characterized as transient state per CLAUDE.md section 4.2. The Modal-before-Global ordering (input_handler.py:117-119) and modal-stack reset symmetry (app_controller.py:59 push, line 75 pop) are accurately documented. The qualified-baseline section contains a real commit hash (a26f7fb), not a placeholder. v0.2 revision_summary corrections are all valid against the source. Recommend.

TU-UI domain review (REQ-FS-UI-001, REQ-FS-UI-002, REQ-FS-ARCH-001, REQ-FS-ARCH-004, REQ-FS-CAD-003): all cited line numbers and class/method names verified against flow-state@a26f7fb. UIManager:70, OverlayProvider:31, Dropdown:712, register_overlay/_draw_overlays at 301/330 confirmed. Runtime dispatch order in input_handler.handle_input (lines 113-129) matches the System->Modal->Global->HUD ordering required by REQ-FS-UI-002, with reset_interaction_state correctly invoked from both push_modal (app_controller.py:59) and pop_modal (app_controller.py:75). ToolContext facade methods at the cited line ranges (paint/erase 383-413, set/clear interaction_data 427-458) verified. The honest disclosures throughout the document are appropriate and well-scoped: (a) the brush 'authorized exception' for REQ-FS-ARCH-001 is correctly framed against CLAUDE.md section 3.3's time-travel snapshot pattern; (b) the unused set_interaction_data facade is a latent inconsistency but is transparently disclosed and does not violate the persistent-state Air Gap; (c) the stale 5-layer docstring at input_handler.py:4-9 and CLAUDE.md section 6.2 narrative is acknowledged with a queued follow-up CR. The qualified baseline commit a26f7fb is real (not a placeholder) and matches the current flow-state submodule HEAD. Recommend.

Reviewed v0.2 against the qualified baseline (flow-state @ a26f7fb). All CAD/Geometry-domain evidence verified accurate: Scene.execute at scene.py:184 (REQ-FS-ARCH-001); Command ABC abstract methods + historize at command_base.py and RemoveEntityCommand at model/commands/geometry.py:77 (REQ-FS-ARCH-002); Scene/Session attribute split confirmed (REQ-FS-ARCH-003); ToolContext facade with brush at tool_context.py:383-413 and set_interaction_data at 427-458 (REQ-FS-ARCH-004); constraint classes Coincident/Collinear/Length/Radius/EqualLength/Angle/FixedAngle/Midpoint exist in model/constraints.py with HORIZONTAL/VERTICAL as Angle variants per constraint_factory.py:105/113 (REQ-FS-CAD-001); Solver.solve at solver.py:18 dispatching to solve_numba at line 55, with unary_types set at line 35 and _solve_interaction at line 42, F9 toggle at input_handler.py:197 (REQ-FS-CAD-002); tools mutate sketch.interaction_data directly at tools.py:788/950/978/1061 with the facade existing but unused as RTM honestly notes (REQ-FS-CAD-003); Compiler tethered atom is_static=3 at compiler.py:236 and static is_static=1 at line 266, Scene.rebuild at scene.py:421 (REQ-FS-SIM-002). The v0.2 revision substantively addressed prior feedback: paint/erase exception now correctly documented as the time-travel snapshot pattern per CLAUDE.md s3.3, and the architectural honesty about Sketch-owned interaction_data, the unused ToolContext facade, and the stale input_handler docstring (flagged for follow-up CR) are appropriate. Architectural framing is sound and aligns with the SoC between Sketch and Simulation domains. Recommend approval.
